This happens to be my 2007 coaching call schedule, which is in that, gee, it seemed like a good idea to fit it on one day at the time. But, yeah, what's kind of important about this is, to mention to you, is that they don't pick. That will drive you nuts. You start to run any kind of a democracy approach to running coaching groups where they're getting to pick slots, pick who goes in what order at your meetings, any of that kind of stuff. This is all assigned. So, when they join a group or when we renew a group or we're getting a group organized for the year, they are allowed to express their preferences, and then I ultimately assign them their time slots. Now, I try and be sensitive to their preferences. I try and be sensitive to time zones, but I also am very sensitive to my own priorities. So, for example, on a day like this, I know where my energy laws are going to be. I put people there intentionally who I know are going to pick me up, not be difficult, or dull, or whatever. I know a few people who are going to miss their calls two times during the year, three times a year, four times a year, they're going adjacent to a break. I don't care what time slot they want it, because if they haven't called in in the first five minutes, odds are they ain't calling in any other 15. Guess what? I just doubled my break time. All right. So, and I got to put real sharp pencils at the end, because I'm like dying at the end of the day. So, I got to have people there that those calls are a real pleasure and they're really organized and they got a lot going on. So, it just as an example, it affects everything that you do. You've got to take into consideration the things that they want, but ultimately you build it to suit you. Doesn't matter whether it's a call schedule, it's a meeting, it's an agenda, you know, whatever. You build it to suit you. I'm not sure why I had that first, but this is, this goes back a ways. If you flip back, we're jumping around. If you flip back to page 131, you're going to see a page like, looks like this. Now, this was, this was from the OVIP groups. And again, we gave this to them and let them give first preference, second preference, third preference, got all their preferences in, some consideration to first come, first served, but again, ultimately building it to suit me more than to suit anybody else. I'll tell you, for example, if you look at page 156, here's the 2006 list for day two. There were two days back to back. The second one needed a lot of help and was sort of the weak link of the group. I mean, no disrespect. It's just what was sort of the weak link of the group. So, I had to put her between two people I was really good at talking to and we're going to make me work too hard and we're going to come to the table with a lot of good stuff. If I put three weak links together, I'm irritable by the time I'm dealing with the third one. It comes through in your voice. You know, you're liable not to deal with them well, you know, et cetera, et cetera. Again, there's one on here. I guess I won't point them out, but there's one on here who pretty much missed his call almost every month and had done so the previous year. So, he got put next to a break, which buys me, you know, a bigger break. Page 157, here's for example a document. Here's part of a fulfillment document. So, this is private client group. So, this is an 06 private client group fulfillment memo.
